mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate keeps the discount rate applied to equity cash flows compressed, sustaining a positive drift in equity valuations. Continuous low FEDFUNDS levels are associated with above-median 20-day forward SPX returns across the sample.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or mean forward return in low-rate periods is not statistically greater than in high-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recessionary risk, causing investors to discount future earnings more heavily and driving SPX lower over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following a triggered UNRATE spike are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or the effect is not significant (p > 0.05) versus non-triggered baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength, which jointly reinforces expectations of corporate revenue growth and produces positive SPX returns over the following month. Neither series alone captures the full demand-side signal; their co-movement is required.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to produce forward 20d returns significantly higher than episodes where only one series accelerates (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the joint signal is lower than the best single-series IC.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, decelerating industrial production growth foreshadows margin compression and earnings disappointments, leading to negative SPX returns over the following quarter. This relationship is expected to be much weaker or absent in early-cycle recoveries where INDPRO deceleration reflects normalization rather than distress.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration predicts negative 60d forward SPX returns with the same or greater strength during early-cycle periods as during late-cycle periods, invalidating the regime dependence; or Rank IC < 0.03 in late-cycle subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects ongoing labor market expansion and consumer income growth, supporting near-term equity risk appetite and producing modest positive SPX drift over the following week.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward SPX return conditional on positive PAYEMS growth is not significantly different from unconditional mean (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising simultaneously with accelerating CPI inflation, markets price in an aggressive tightening path that compresses valuation multiples, producing negative SPX returns over the following month. The interaction of both rising rates and rising inflation is required; either alone is insufficient to trigger the repricing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS up over trailing 3 months AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes do not produce significantly more negative forward returns than episodes with only rate increases or only inflation increases (ANOVA p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below a critical threshold signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, historically a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, driving negative SPX returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over trailing 6 months
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST decline episodes show SPX 60d forward returns positive in more than 50% of instances, or effect size is not statistically distinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in industrial production and payroll growth signals a synchronized supply-and-labor slowdown that markets have not yet fully priced, generating a short-term negative surprise reaction in SPX over the following week. Individually, mild deceleration in either series is common and non-informative; only their joint occurrence carries predictive signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change < 0 AND PAYEMS MoM change < 100k
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to produce forward 5d SPX returns more negative than the unconditional mean (p > 0.10), or single-series deceleration alone explains equivalent predictive power (interaction term not significant in regression).
